msgs.text:
Turn 89 — final stop-rule: freeze the proof, do not reopen the sweep I re-read the three attachments from this message only. The required history file is present, and the latest recorded state is Turn 88. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH, equivalently whether there is a continuous isomorphism commuting with the canonical projections to G. The attached history’s Turn 88 already freezes the proof at the stronger F=P⋊G level: construct a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F withΦ(rS−1)=rH,then descend to the one-relator quotients.The correct Turn 89 move is not to open another Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, or finite exceptional case. Turn 88 explicitly says the obstruction sweep is closed and that only external reproducibility/presentation artifacts remain for a standalone manuscript.

Final frozen theorem

There exists a continuous automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊G over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Therefore Φ descends toΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F=ΓH.

So the answer to the original problem is:ΓS≅GΓH.

Why no further case sweep is appropriate The final proof is a certificate theorem, not a search for one more obstruction. The certificate chain is:

Fixed orientation: useρS:=rS−1.H1 -Nielsen equivalence: the right Λ=Z2 [[G]]-rowswS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E)are related by a global U∈GL2 (Λ). On the dangerous Ef-piece, with s=σ2,(s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),detU0=s,a unit. This fixes the first Magnus layer.

Residual restricted-Lie induction: after the H1 -change, non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled, and the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 problem reduces toS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2.

The obstruction quotient has the uniform formLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even,and the sweep proves Cm=Lm for m≥5, so every residual layer dies uniformly.Finite nonlinear certificates D.1–D.4: the only exceptional finite nonlinear blocks are frozen as:ker∂8↠L5[2],the residual degree-16 lower-base λ-toggle,rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61,andθ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],λ((B8)[2])=1.These certificates kill the fixed-gauge exceptional classes rather than creating new cases.Normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction: on the local Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,the raw row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t),because (s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2. After that normalization, the coefficient tower is killed by the regular Koszul row (2,t) and the Magnus-degree-first Witt cascade.Finite filtered Nielsen compactness: for coefficient-and-degree truncationsPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM),the finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle solution sets SN,J are nonempty, finite, and functorial under refinement. Compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence a continuous over-G automorphism Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g with Φ(rS−1)=rH.The remaining work is therefore only manuscript packaging: include verifier scripts, pivot tables, or explicit matrices for the finite certificate blocks in a standalone appendix. That is an external reproducibility boundary, not a mathematical gap in the attached sweep.

ESTABLISHED: The attached Turn 88 certificate theorem is frozen: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. Hence Φ identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends to ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. Only external reproducibility/presentation artifacts remain for a standalone paper: verifier scripts, pivot tables, or explicit finite matrices for Certificates D.1, D.2, and the D.3 stack-rank 60→61.

NEXT: Stop the obstruction sweep. Prepare the final manuscript appendix in frozen certificate form: right-row convention, Certificates 0–F, finite verifier outputs, nonlinear blocks D.1–D.4, normalized Koszul–Witt induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN89-FINAL-STOP-RULE-CERTIFICATE-FREEZE-ISO]
